overview: This fungus appears to resemble a stinkhorn, potentially the Dog Stinkhorn (Mutinus caninus) or a similar species in its early or "egg" stage. Stinkhorn fungi are known for emerging from egg-like structures and often have a gelatinous, slimy tip that attracts flies to spread their spores. Another possibility is a white, misshapen fungus such as Aborted Entoloma (Entoloma abortivum), which can appear as firm, irregular white globs. www.creativesustenance.com +1 Identification Note: Accurately identifying wild mushrooms can be difficult from a single photo; never eat a wild mushroom without 100% certainty of its identification by an expert, as many white fungi are poisonous or inedible. Wild Mushroom Hunting +1
